name: Poly CV
description: This algorithm takes the polysynth control logic from the other polysynths and uses it not to produce sound, but to output CVs and gates. This makes it most obviously useful as a MIDI/CV converter, but it can also be used as a chord/arpeggio engine controlled by CV/gate inputs. In fact, the algorithm can also generate MIDI as well as or instead of CV/gates, and so works as a MIDI-to-MIDI chord engine, for example [99].
use_cases: chord/arpeggio engine controlled by CV/gate inputs
gui_description: The display shows the notes being played by the various voices. When adjusting the microtuning parameters, more information about the chosen tuning is displayed.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel to listen on.

parameters.name: MPE channels
parameters.description: Controls how the algorithm will respond to MPE.

parameters.name: I2C channel
parameters.description: Sets the I2C channel.

parameters.name: Sustain mode
parameters.description: The standard polysynth sustain mode parameter.

parameters.name: Sustain
parameters.description: Directly controls the sustain (like a MIDI sustain pedal).

parameters.name: Transpose
parameters.description: Coarse tuning control.

parameters.name: Fine tune
parameters.description: Fine tuning control.

parameters.name: Bend range
parameters.description: The MIDI pitch bend range.

parameters.name: Min vel gate
parameters.description: Sets the minimum gate voltage, if the gates are set to be scaled by velocity. Velocity gates range from this voltage (for MIDI velocity 0) up to 5V (for MIDI velocity 127).

parameters.name: Voice allocation
parameters.description: Sets the voice allocation scheme for polyphonic converters – one of ‘Round robin’ or ‘Lowest voice’.

parameters.name: Voices
parameters.description: The number of voices to use (up to the maximum set by the specification).

parameters.name: Gate outputs
parameters.description: Whether to include gate outputs. If set to ‘Velocity’, the gate outputs are scaled by the note velocity.

parameters.name: Pitch outputs
parameters.description: Whether to include pitch CV outputs.

parameters.name: Velocity outputs
parameters.description: Whether to include velocity outputs.

parameters.name: Gate mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above for gate outputs.

parameters.name: Pitch mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above for pitch CV outputs.

parameters.name: Velocity mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above for velocity outputs.

parameters.name: ES-5 Expander
parameters.description: If set, the ES-5 expander header to use for the gate output instead of allocating a gate from the regular busses. “1” means the ES-5 itself.

parameters.name: Paraphonic gate
parameters.description: The output bus for a paraphonic gate (which is high while any voice gate output is high).

parameters.name: Para gate mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above for the paraphonic gate output.

parameters.name: Apply global tuning
parameters.description: If enabled, apply the global tuning setting to the output pitch CVs. Disable this when using the pitch CVs internally to the NT to control another algorithm, since the controlled algorithm will be using the global tuning setting to adjust its pitch.

parameters.name: MIDI channel (Output)
parameters.description: The output MIDI channel.

parameters.name: Output to breakout
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the breakout.

parameters.name: Output to Select Bus
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the Select Bus.

parameters.name: Output to USB
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to USB.

parameters.name: Output to internal
parameters.description: Enables internal MIDI output – that is, MIDI is sent to the other algorithms.

input_ports.name: CV/gate inputs
input_ports.description: Inputs used to control the chord/arpeggio engine.

output_ports.name: Pitch CV
output_ports.description: Pitch CV output for each voice.

output_ports.name: First output
output_ports.description: The first output bus to use.

output_ports.name: ES-5 Output
output_ports.description: If using an ES-5 expander output, sets which of the 8 outputs on the expander to use.
